LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT BINH HOANG'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT IS CONTINUED TO MAY 1, 2014 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302, TO GIVE THE OPPOSING PARTY A CHANCE TO COMPLY WITH SAN FRANCISCO LOCAL RULE 2.6B. THE OPPOSING PARTY SHALL IMMEDIATELY PROVIDE A COURTESY COPY OF THE OPPOSITION WITH A COVER LETTER REFLECTING THE NEW HEARING DATE. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED. (302)
MINI MINUTES FOR MAY-01-2014 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302
ORDER DENYING DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO VACATE JUDGMENT

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED THE TENTATIVE RULING WHICH WAS NOT CONTESTED. DEFENDANT BINH HOANG'S MOTION TO VACATE JUDGMENT PURSUANT TO CCP 473(B) IS DENIED. THE MANDATORY RELIEF PROVISIONS OF CCP 473 DO NOT APPLY TO DEFENDANT BECAUSE DEFENDANT IS SEEKING RELIEF FROM AN ORDER GRANTING SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND JUDGMENT ENTERED THEREON. SEE ENGLISH V. IKON BUSINESS SOLUTIONS, INC., (2001) 94 CAL. APP. 4TH 130, 138; PRIETO V. LOYOLA MARYMOUNT UNIV. (2005) 132 CAL.APP.4TH 290, 297; LAS VEGAS LAND AND DEVELOPMENT COMPANY, LLC V. WILKIE WAY, LLC (2013) 219 CAL.APP.4TH 1086. "A SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS NEITHER A 'DEFAULT,' NOR A 'DEFAULT JUDGMENT,' NOR A 'DISMISSAL' WITHIN THE MEANING OF SECTION 473(B)." ENGLISH V. IKON BUSINESS SOLUTIONS, INC., SUPRA, 94 CAL. APP. 4TH AT 133. DEFENDANT'S MOTION APPEARS TO BE BASED SOLELY ON THE MANDATORY PROVISIONS OF CCP 473. IN THE LAST SENTENCE OF ITS REPLY BRIEF, DEFENDANT STATES THAT EVEN IF THE MANDATORY PROVISION IS NOT APPLICABLE, "DISCRETIONARY RELIEF IS STILL AVAILABLE TO DEFENDANT." REPLY BR. AT 3. HOWEVER, DEFENDANT FAILS TO ESTABLISH ANY FACTUAL BASIS FOR RELIEF UNDER THE DISCRETIONARY PORTIONS OF CCP 473. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR# 5976.

calendar.matters:
MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT
Off Calendar May-01-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted the tentative ruling which was not contested. DEFENDANT BINH HOANG'S MOTION TO VACATE JUDGMENT PURSUANT TO CCP 473(b) is denied. The mandatory relief provisions of CCP 473 do not apply to defendant because defendant is seeking relief from an order granting summary judgment and judgment entered thereon. See English v. IKON Business Solutions, Inc., (2001) 94 Cal. App. 4th 130, 138; Prieto v. Loyola Marymount Univ. (2005) 132 Cal.App.4th 290, 297; Las Vegas Land and Development Company, LLC v. Wilkie Way, LLC (2013) 219 Cal.App.4th 1086. "A summary judgment is neither a 'default,' nor a 'default judgment,' nor a 'dismissal' within the meaning of section 473(b)." English v. IKON Business Solutions, Inc., supra, 94 Cal. App. 4th at 133. Defendant's motion appears to be based solely on the mandatory provisions of CCP 473. In the last sentence of its reply brief, Defendant states that even if the mandatory provision is not applicable, "discretionary relief is still available to Defendant." Reply Br. at 3. However, Defendant fails to establish any factual basis for relief under the discretionary portions of CCP 473. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR# 5976.

MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Apr-01-2014 Continued To May-01-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT BINH HOANG'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT is continued to May 1, 2014, to give the opposing party a chance to comply with San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B. The opposing party shall immediately provide a courtesy copy of the opposition with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.
